errors.yearInPast: We can't check the stock market for old years! {{year}} is in the past.
errors.yearTooFarFuture: We don't know about more than 20 years from now! {{year}} is too far in the future.

checks.taxReportCount: Expected 20 tax reports (one per year) but got {{got}}. Make sure you call reportTax() inside the loop.
checks.taxReportWrongYear: Tax report #{{index}} was for year {{actualYear}} but should have been for year {{expectedYear}}.
checks.taxReportWrongBalance: You reported the wrong tax for {{year}}. It should have been ${{expected}} but was ${{actual}}.
checks.wrongFinalBalance: Expected a final balance of approximately ${{expected}} but got ${{got}}. Make sure you call announceToFamily() after the loop.
checks.announcedMultipleTimes: You should only announce to your family once, after the 20 years are up, not every year. You called announceToFamily() {{count}} {{times}}.

growInvestment.name: Grow your investment over 20 years
growInvestment.description: Start with $10, simulate 20 years of stock market growth, report to the taxman each year, and announce the final balance to your family.

twentyYears.name: 20 years of growth
twentyYears.description: Invest $10 and track your balance over 20 years of market growth.

whereToStart.question: I don't know where to start
whereToStart.answer: You need to use `marketGrowth()` for each year over the next 20 years (starting from now). That will return a value like 5 (for 5% market growth). You then need to track how much money you have - from the $10 you started changed by whatever the interest rate is.

stillStuck.question: I'm still really stuck - I don't get it.

formula.question: What's the formula to track the growth? I can't work it out myself.
formula.answer:
The formula is: 

```javascript
money = money * (100 + marketGrowth(year)) / 100
```

wrongTaxReportCount.question: It says I've not reported the right amount of tax returns.
wrongTaxReportCount.answer: You should report 20 tax returns for 20 years. Use the scrubber to count how many times you use the `reportTax()` function.

describers.marketGrowth: got market growth of ${return}% for year ${arg1}

marketGrowth.description: Returns the market growth percentage for a given year (between -25 and 40). You'll need to **increase** the amount of money you have by this number each time. (So 40 means that your $10 should be worth $14, and -10 means your $10 goes down to $9)

reportTax.description: Reports your tax for a given year. You should call this every year, with the year and the current amount of money you have.

announceToFamily.description: Announces your final balance to your family.
